Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Form Submissions Viewer + Resource Vault File Attachments

## Context
The client portal needs a "Form Submissions" section so clients can review their past form responses, and the Resource Vault should also surface any files they uploaded through forms.

## File to Modify
- `app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx` ÔÇö all changes in this single file

## Key Data Model Facts
- `form_responses`: `id, form_id, response_data (JSONB), submitted_by_email, submitted_by_name, submitted_at, client_id`
- `forms`: `id, name, slug, status, ...`
- `form_fields`: `id, form_id, label, field_type, page_number, sort_order, allow_attachments, ...`
- File uploads stored in `response_data` as `${fieldId}_attachments: [url1, url2, ...]`
- Existing code selects `responses` from `form_responses` ÔÇö column is `response_data`; new code will use `response_data`
- Existing `stripHtml()` utility already available

## Step 1: Types & State

Add type:
```ts
type FormSubmission = {
  id: string;
  formName: string;
  submittedAt: string;
  fields: { label: string; answer: string }[];
  attachments: { label: string; url: string; fileName: string }[];
};
```

Add state:
```ts
const [formSubmissions, setFormSubmissions] = useState<FormSubmission[]>([]);
const [viewingSubmission, setViewingSubmission] = useState<FormSubmission | null>(null);
```

## Step 2: `fetchFormSubmissions()`

- Query `form_responses` where `client_id = clientId`, get `id, form_id, response_data, submitted_at`
- Get unique form IDs, batch-fetch `forms(id, name)` and `form_fields(id, form_id, label, field_type, page_number, sort_order)` (exclude section/description)
- For each response:
  - Map field IDs Ôćĺ labels/answers (skip empty, use `stripHtml` on labels)
  - Extract `*_attachments` keys Ôćĺ build attachments array with label, url, derived fileName
- Sort by `submitted_at` desc
- Call from main `useEffect` alongside other fetches

## Step 3: Render "Form Submissions" Section

Position: after Decision Log, before Resources

- Card with icon header (ClipboardList or FileText icon, orange gradient)
- List of submissions as clickable rows: form name, submission date, field count badge
- Click opens a Dialog:
  - Header: form name + submission date
  - Body: Q&A pairs in styled rows (label Ôćĺ answer)
  - Attachments section at bottom: file cards with icon + download link
- Only renders if `formSubmissions.length > 0`

## Step 4: Resource Vault ÔÇö Include Uploaded Files

- Collect all attachments from all `formSubmissions` into a flat array
- In the existing Resources card, add a "Uploaded Files" subsection below the link cards
- Each file shown as a small card with file-type icon (PDF=red, image=blue, doc=indigo, other=gray), file name, and external link
- Update the Resources visibility condition: show if any resource URL exists OR any form attachments exist

## Portal Section Order (updated)
1ÔÇô11. (unchanged from previous implementation)
12. **Form Submissions** ÔćÉ NEW
13. Resources (now also shows uploaded files from forms)
14. Footer

## Verification
1. Client with form submissions Ôćĺ "Form Submissions" card appears
2. Click a submission Ôćĺ dialog shows Q&A pairs and any attached files
3. Files uploaded in forms Ôćĺ appear in Resources under "Uploaded Files"
4. No submissions / no files Ôćĺ respective sections hidden

   285Ôćĺ  // Check if user is already authenticated via cache
   286Ôćĺ  const checkCachedAuth = () => {
   287Ôćĺ    if (!clientEmail) return;
   288Ôćĺ
   289Ôćĺ    try {
   290Ôćĺ      const cached = localStorage.getItem(cacheKey);
   291Ôćĺ      if (cached) {
   292Ôćĺ        const { email: cachedEmail, timestamp } = JSON.parse(cached);
   293Ôćĺ        const now = Date.now();
   294Ôćĺ
   295Ôćĺ        if (now - timestamp < CACHE_DURATION) {
   296Ôćĺ          if (cachedEmail && cachedEmail.toLowerCase() === clientEmail.toLowerCase()) {
   297Ôćĺ            setEmail(cachedEmail);
   298Ôćĺ            setIsAuthenticated(true); // Start loading data immediately
   299Ôćĺ            setWelcomePhase('enter');
   300Ôćĺ            setShowWelcome(true);
   301Ôćĺ            requestAnimationFrame(() => {
   302Ôćĺ              setTimeout(() => setWelcomePhase('ready'), 50);
   303Ôćĺ            });
   304Ôćĺ            return;
   305Ôćĺ          }
   306Ôćĺ        }
   307Ôćĺ        localStorage.removeItem(cacheKey);
   308Ôćĺ      }
   309Ôćĺ    } catch (error) {
   310Ôćĺ      console.error('Error checking cached auth:', error);
   311Ôćĺ      localStorage.removeItem(cacheKey);
   312Ôćĺ    }
   313Ôćĺ  };
   314Ôćĺ
   315Ôćĺ  // Save authentication to cache
   316Ôćĺ  const saveAuthToCache = (email: string) => {

   319Ôćĺ        email,
   320Ôćĺ        clientId,
   321Ôćĺ        timestamp: Date.now()
   322Ôćĺ      };
   323Ôćĺ      localStorage.setItem(cacheKey, JSON.stringify(authData));
   324Ôćĺ      // Save global portal auth for cross-page navigation (campaign/report pages)
   325Ôćĺ      const globalAuthData = {
   326Ôćĺ        email,
   327Ôćĺ        clientId,
   328Ôćĺ        clientEmail,
   329Ôćĺ        timestamp: Date.now()
   330Ôćĺ      };
   331Ôćĺ      localStorage.setItem('portal_global_auth', JSON.stringify(globalAuthData));
   332Ôćĺ    } catch (error) {
   333Ôćĺ      console.error('Error saving auth to cache:', error);
   334Ôćĺ    }
   335Ôćĺ  };
   336Ôćĺ
   337Ôćĺ  const handleEmailSubmit = async (e: React.FormEvent) => {
   338Ôćĺ    e.preventDefault();
   339Ôćĺ    setEmailError('');
   340Ôćĺ
   341Ôćĺ    const emailRegex = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/;
   342Ôćĺ    if (!emailRegex.test(email)) {

   546Ôćĺ  async function fetchCampaignDNA(formId: string) {
   547Ôćĺ    if (!clientId) return;
   548Ôćĺ    try {
   549Ôćĺ      // Fetch form fields (exclude section/description types)
   550Ôćĺ      const { data: fields } = await supabasePublic
   551Ôćĺ        .from('form_fields')
   552Ôćĺ        .select('id, label, field_type, page_number, sort_order')
   553Ôćĺ        .eq('form_id', formId)
   554Ôćĺ        .not('field_type', 'in', '("section","description")')
   555Ôćĺ        .order('page_number', { ascending: true })
   556Ôćĺ        .order('sort_order', { ascending: true });
   557Ôćĺ
   558Ôćĺ      if (!fields || fields.length === 0) return;
   559Ôćĺ
   560Ôćĺ      // Fetch the latest response for this client
   561Ôćĺ      const { data: responses } = await supabasePublic
   562Ôćĺ        .from('form_responses')
   563Ôćĺ        .select('id, responses')
   564Ôćĺ        .eq('form_id', formId)
   565Ôćĺ        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   566Ôćĺ        .order('submitted_at', { ascending: false })
   567Ôćĺ        .limit(1);
   568Ôćĺ
   569Ôćĺ      if (!responses || responses.length === 0) return;
   570Ôćĺ
   571Ôćĺ      const responseData = responses[0].responses as Record<string, any>;
   572Ôćĺ      const dnaFields: CampaignDNAField[] = [];
   573Ôćĺ
   574Ôćĺ      for (const field of fields) {
   575Ôćĺ        const answer = responseData[field.id];
   576Ôćĺ        if (answer === undefined || answer === null || answer === '') continue;
   577Ôćĺ        const answerStr = Array.isArray(answer) ? answer.join(', ') : String(answer);
   578Ôćĺ        if (!answerStr.trim()) continue;
   579Ôćĺ        dnaFields.push({
   580Ôćĺ          label: stripHtml(field.label),
   581Ôćĺ          answer: answerStr,
   582Ôćĺ          page_number: field.page_number || 1,
   583Ôćĺ        });
   584Ôćĺ      }
   585Ôćĺ
   586Ôćĺ      setCampaignDNA(dnaFields);
   587Ôćĺ    } catch (err) {
   588Ôćĺ      console.error('Error fetching campaign DNA:', err);
   589Ôćĺ    }
   590Ôćĺ  }
   591Ôćĺ

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-12 04:37:34Z)**_

All sections are in the correct order: Decision Log Ôćĺ Form Submissions Ôćĺ Resources (with Uploaded Files) Ôćĺ Footer.

Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**Step 1 - Types & State:** Added `FormSubmission` type and `formSubmissions`/`viewingSubmission` state variables.

**Step 2 - `fetchFormSubmissions()`:** Queries `form_responses` by `client_id`, batch-fetches `forms` and `form_fields`, maps field IDs to Q&A pairs, extracts `*_attachments` keys into an attachments array. Called from the main data-fetching `useEffect`.

**Step 3 - Form Submissions section:** Renders after Decision Log as a card with orange gradient icon header. Each submission is a clickable row showing form name, date, and field/file count badges. Clicking opens a Dialog with Q&A pairs (orange left-border style) and an attachments grid with file-type icons and download links. Hidden when no submissions exist.

**Step 4 - Resource Vault uploaded files:** Collects all attachments from all form submissions into `formAttachments`. Resources section visibility updated to also show when form attachments exist. "Uploaded Files" subsection renders below the link cards with file-type colored icons (PDF=red, image=blue, doc=indigo, other=gray).

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is clear. The `form_responses` SELECT policy "Clients can check their own responses" has `qual: (client_id IS NOT NULL)` - this allows reading any response where client_id is not null. That should work.

But the `forms` table only allows viewing published forms via the anon key. So if any forms are not published (e.g., archived or draft), they won't show up in the forms query.

Let me also check the actual column name - the existing code uses `responses` but the submit route uses `response_data`. Let me verify the actual column.
</details></think>

The column is `response_data`. The RLS policy on `form_responses` allows SELECT where `client_id IS NOT NULL` ÔÇö that's fine for anon access. Let me also check ÔÇö the portal uses `supabasePublic` (anon key), so the forms query will only return published ones. That's the issue for form names, but the responses themselves should load.

1. **`sort_order` Ôćĺ `display_order`** ÔÇö the `form_fields` table uses `display_order`, not `sort_order`. This was causing the fields query to fail silently, so no Q&A pairs were being built (only attachments from raw key scanning worked).

2. **`responses` Ôćĺ `response_data`** ÔÇö the existing `fetchCampaignDNA` was also using the wrong column name. Fixed that too so Campaign DNA will now work properly.

3. **Added "Submitted Forms" subheading** above the form list inside the Form Submissions card, matching the "Uploaded Files" subheading style in the Resources card.

_**User (2026-03-12 06:15:47Z)**_

The Core Strategy: A "Dynamic" Welcome Screen
Because you have clients who delay forms (Xyber) and clients who speed-run them (Fogo), your welcome screen must have two distinct visual states:
State A (Pre-Form Submission): The screen acts as a high-end "getting started" portal. The primary call-to-action (CTA) is completing the form, accompanied by a visual timeline of what happens immediately after they hit submit.
State B (Post-Form Submission): The screen instantly transforms into a "Command Center." It reorganizes their submitted data into a clean brief and shifts focus to Holo HiveÔÇÖs internal deliverables (DD, audit, KOL shortlist).
Recommended Screen Components
Here is how you can lay out the interface to maximize client experience and minimize anxiety.
1. The "You Are Here" Timeline (Interactive 7-Day Roadmap)
Instead of just a static list, build a visual tracker (similar to a pizza delivery tracker) that outlines the 7-day communicated timeline.
Day 1: Kickoff & Briefing (Checkbox fills when form is submitted)
Day 2-3: Deep-Dive Audit & Due Diligence (Highlights Holo Hive's active behind-the-scenes work)
Day 4: Strategy & GTM Sync
Day 5-7: KOL Shortlist & Tracker Deployment
Why this works: It visually reinforces Holo Hive's control of the pace. If a client like Xyber lingers on Day 1, they can clearly see that Days 2-7 are locked until they act.
2. The "Action Board" (Who owes what?)
Clear accountability prevents communication gaps. Use a simple two-column or split-card design.
Your Court (Client): E.g., "Submit graphic materials vault," "Approve KOL shortlist."
Our Court (Holo Hive): E.g., "Conducting Product Audit (Expected: Wednesday)," "Drafting GTM Plan."
Why this works: It gently but firmly chases the client for assets without requiring Jdot or Bolt to send nagging Telegram messages.
3. The "Campaign DNA" (Reorganized Form)
As you suggested, once they submit the form, do not just spit the raw data back at them. Parse it into a beautiful, summarized "Campaign Snapshot."
Core Objective: (e.g., Post-TGE Distribution)
Target Audience: (e.g., Korean retail crypto)
Key Selling Points: (Summarized from their input)
Why this works: It demonstrates active listening. It proves Holo Hive immediately ingested their data and is already framing it for the KOLs.
4. The Resource Vault (Centralized Links)
Telegram is great for speed, but links get buried fast. The bottom of the welcome screen should house persistent links.
HHP Campaign Tracker: (Link updates from "Pending" to "Live" around Day 4/5)
Shared Asset Drive: (Where they drop their graphics/briefs)
Telegram Group: (A quick link to launch the main comms channel)
GTM Sync Invite: (Link to the weekly meeting)

UX / Client Experience Tips for the Tech Team
Deploy Instantly: Trigger the creation of this page the moment Jdot drops the welcome message in Telegram. The link should be included in that very first message: "Welcome! WeÔÇÖve set up your Week 1 Command Center here: [Link]. Your first step is inside."
Keep it Read-Only (Mostly): Aside from uploading files or filling out the initial form, the client shouldn't be clicking around trying to edit things. It should feel like a polished presentation of real-time data, not a complicated SaaS tool they need to learn.
Emphasize Holo Hive's Autonomy: To solve the "Xyber" problem, ensure the dashboard shows Holo Hive moving forward even if the client is slow. For example, if the form is delayed, the "Our Court" section should still show: "Initial website review in progress," proving you aren't just sitting on your hands.



The "Flip": Transitioning from Welcome Screen to Tracker
On Day 5 or 6, when the GTM plan is approved and the KOL shortlist is ready, the dashboard undergoes a visual state change.
1. The Timeline Archives (but stays visible)
What happens: The 7-Day "You Are Here" onboarding tracker collapses into a small, completed module at the top or side of the screen (e.g., "Ôťů Onboarding Complete").
Why it works: It subtly reminds the client that Holo Hive successfully delivered on its first promise.
2. The "Action Board" Evolves into "Live Status"
What happens: The "Your Court / Our Court" section transforms into a high-level campaign health monitor.
New Metrics: * KOLs Pitched vs. KOLs Secured.
Content Drafts Pending Review (puts the ball back in the client's court if they need to approve scripts/posts).
Total Deliverables Live.
3. The Core Feature: The Interactive KOL Roster
This becomes the centerpiece of the dashboard. Instead of a messy spreadsheet, build a clean, trackable table.
Columns to Include:
KOL Name & Platform (with links to their profiles).
Tier/Category (e.g., Alpha Caller, Broadcaster, YouTube Deep Dive).
Current Status: Use clear, color-coded tags (e.g., Pitching, Negotiating, Content Creation, Client Review, LIVE, Paid).
Live Link: A clickable button that goes straight to the published content once it's live.
Performance (Optional/V2): If your tech allows, pulling in basic API data (views/likes) later on would make this dashboard incredibly sticky.
4. The "Campaign DNA" Becomes the North Star
What happens: The summarized brief (Target Audience, Key Selling Points) created from their onboarding form remains pinned or easily accessible via a tab.
Why it works: It ensures that if the client ever questions the angle of a KOL's post, the agreed-upon strategy is sitting right there on the same screen to justify Holo Hive's direction.
5. The Resource Vault Expands
What happens: The links to the Telegram chat and shared drives stay, but you add sections, eventually, End of Campaign Reports.
The Technical Approach: "Progressive Disclosure"
For your tech team, the development principle here is "Progressive Disclosure." They don't need to build two separate apps. They build one app with three permission/time-based states:
State 1 (Day 1): Form input + "What to expect" timeline.
State 2 (Days 2-5): Reorganized brief + "We are doing DD" status + KOL Shortlist Approval.
State 3 (Day 6 onwards): The full Campaign Tracker unlocks.


Is there anything else we did not do from this doc?

   431Ôćĺ  async function fetchCampaigns() {
   432Ôćĺ    if (!clientId) return;

   434Ôćĺ      setLoading(true);

   437Ôćĺ      const { data: campaignsData, error: campaignsError } = await supabasePublic
   438Ôćĺ        .from('campaigns')
   439Ôćĺ        .select(`
   440Ôćĺ          id,
   441Ôćĺ          name,
   442Ôćĺ          slug,
   443Ôćĺ          status,
   444Ôćĺ          total_budget,
   445Ôćĺ          start_date,
   446Ôćĺ          end_date,
   447Ôćĺ          region,
   448Ôćĺ          description,

   670Ôćĺ  async function fetchFormSubmissions() {

   673Ôćĺ      const { data: responsesData } = await supabasePublic

   678Ôćĺ
   679Ôćĺ      if (!responsesData || responsesData.length === 0) return;
   680Ôćĺ
   681Ôćĺ      const formIds = [...new Set(responsesData.map(r => r.form_id))];
   682Ôćĺ
   683Ôćĺ      const [{ data: formsData }, { data: fieldsData }] = await Promise.all([
   684Ôćĺ        supabasePublic
   685Ôćĺ          .from('forms')
   686Ôćĺ          .select('id, name')
   687Ôćĺ          .in('id', formIds),
   688Ôćĺ        supabasePublic
   689Ôćĺ          .from('form_fields')
   690Ôćĺ          .select('id, form_id, label, field_type, page_number, display_order')
   691Ôćĺ          .in('form_id', formIds)
   692Ôćĺ          .not('field_type', 'in', '("section","description")')
   693Ôćĺ          .order('page_number', { ascending: true })
   694Ôćĺ          .order('display_order', { ascending: true }),
   695Ôćĺ      ]);
   696Ôćĺ
   697Ôćĺ      const formNameMap = new Map((formsData || []).map(f => [f.id, f.name]));
   698Ôćĺ      const fieldsByForm = new Map<string, typeof fieldsData>();
   699Ôćĺ      for (const field of fieldsData || []) {
   700Ôćĺ        const arr = fieldsByForm.get(field.form_id) || [];
   701Ôćĺ        arr.push(field);
   702Ôćĺ        fieldsByForm.set(field.form_id, arr);
   703Ôćĺ      }
   704Ôćĺ
   705Ôćĺ      const submissions: FormSubmission[] = responsesData.map(resp => {
   706Ôćĺ        const rd = (resp.response_data || {}) as Record<string, any>;
   707Ôćĺ        const fields = fieldsByForm.get(resp.form_id) || [];
   708Ôćĺ        const qaPairs: { label: string; answer: string }[] = [];
   709Ôćĺ        const attachments: { label: string; url: string; fileName: string }[] = [];
   710Ôćĺ
   711Ôćĺ        for (const field of fields) {
   712Ôćĺ          const answer = rd[field.id];
   713Ôćĺ          if (answer === undefined || answer === null || answer === '') continue;
   714Ôćĺ          const answerStr = Array.isArray(answer) ? answer.join(', ') : String(answer);
   715Ôćĺ          if (!answerStr.trim()) continue;
   716Ôćĺ          qaPairs.push({ label: stripHtml(field.label), answer: answerStr });
   717Ôćĺ        }
   718Ôćĺ
   719Ôćĺ        // Extract attachments
   720Ôćĺ        for (const key of Object.keys(rd)) {
   721Ôćĺ          if (key.endsWith('_attachments') && Array.isArray(rd[key])) {
   722Ôćĺ            const fieldId = key.replace('_attachments', '');
   723Ôćĺ            const field = fields.find(f => f.id === fieldId);
   724Ôćĺ            const label = field ? stripHtml(field.label) : 'Attachment';
   725Ôćĺ            for (const url of rd[key]) {
   726Ôćĺ              if (typeof url === 'string' && url.trim()) {
   727Ôćĺ                const fileName = decodeURIComponent(url.split('/').pop() || 'file');
   728Ôćĺ                attachments.push({ label, url, fileName });
   729Ôćĺ              }
   730Ôćĺ            }
   731Ôćĺ          }
   732Ôćĺ        }
   733Ôćĺ
   734Ôćĺ        return {
   735Ôćĺ          id: resp.id,
   736Ôćĺ          formName: formNameMap.get(resp.form_id) || 'Form',
   737Ôćĺ          submittedAt: resp.submitted_at,
   738Ôćĺ          fields: qaPairs,
   739Ôćĺ          attachments,
   740Ôćĺ        };
   741Ôćĺ      });
   742Ôćĺ
   743Ôćĺ      setFormSubmissions(submissions);
   744Ôćĺ    } catch (err) {

   746Ôćĺ    }
   747Ôćĺ  }
   748Ôćĺ
   749Ôćĺ  // Collect all form attachments for resource vault
   750Ôćĺ  const formAttachments = formSubmissions.flatMap(s => s.attachments);
   751Ôćĺ
   752Ôćĺ  // Filter campaigns based on active tab and search term
   753Ôćĺ  const filteredCampaigns = campaigns.filter(campaign => {
   754Ôćĺ    const matchesTab = activeTab === 'all' ||

## SPECIFICATION COMPLIANCE ANALYSIS

### State A (Pre-Form Submission)

### 1. "You Are Here" Timeline (7-Day Roadmap)
**NOT IMPLEMENTED** - The specification calls for a visual tracker (like pizza delivery tracker) with:
- Day 1: Kickoff & Briefing (checkbox fills when form submitted)
- Day 2-3: Deep-Dive Audit & DD
- Day 4: Strategy & GTM Sync
- Day 5-7: KOL Shortlist & Tracker Deployment
- Progressive locking of days 2-7 if client lingers on Day 1

**CURRENT STATE:** No timeline implementation exists in the portal at all.

---

### 2. "Action Board" (Who owes what?)
**NOT IMPLEMENTED** - No two-column "Your Court (Client)" vs "Our Court (Holo Hive)" board showing action items and deliverables.

- **DONE:** Campaign DNA state exists and is fetched

### Progressive Disclosure States
**NOT IMPLEMENTED** - No state machine for progressive disclosure:

<tool-use data-tool-type="generic" data-tool-name="AskUserQuestion"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **AskUserQuestion**</summary>
**For the 7-Day Timeline, how should we determine which day the client is on? Should it be based on the client's start_date from CRM/client_context, or should we add a new field to track onboarding start?**

Options:
- **Use CRM start_date** - Use the existing start_date from client_context or crm_opportunities closed_at/created_at to calculate which day they're on
- **Use first form submission** - Day 1 starts when the client submits the onboarding form, days before that show as 'pre-kickoff'
- **Use client created_at** - Use the date the client record was created as Day 1

**For the Action Board (Your Court / Our Court), where should the action items come from?**

Options:
- **Hardcoded by state** - Predefined items based on which timeline day/state the client is in (e.g., Day 1 always shows 'Submit onboarding form')
- **Database-driven** - Create a new table (e.g., client_action_items) so your team can manually add/update tasks per client
- **Mix of both** - Hardcoded defaults based on state, plus ability to pull custom items from a DB table if they exist

**For Campaign DNA, do you want to manually map specific form fields to 'Core Objective', 'Target Audience', 'Key Selling Points' labels, or just display the onboarding form responses in a cleaner card format (grouped by page/section)?**

Options:
- **Clean card format** - Show all onboarding form responses grouped by section in a polished card ÔÇö essentially bring back the Campaign Brief but keep it accessible
- **Manual field mapping** - Map specific field IDs to named categories like 'Core Objective', 'Target Audience', etc. Requires knowing which fields correspond to which

**For the progressive disclosure states, should the transition be automatic (time-based from start date) or manually triggered (e.g., a flag your team sets when onboarding is complete)?**

Options:
- **Automatic (time-based)** - States transition automatically based on days since start (Day 1 = State 1, Day 2-5 = State 2, Day 6+ = State 3)
- **Manual trigger** - Add a field like 'onboarding_phase' to client_context that your team updates to control what the client sees
- **Hybrid** - Time-based defaults but your team can override/advance the state manually via a field


**Answer:** Use first form submission, Hardcoded by state, Clean card format, Hybrid
</details></tool-use>

## Client Context Table - Current State Analysis

Based on my thorough investigation of the codebase, here's the complete picture:

### 1. Full Column List for client_context Table

The `client_context` table currently has the following columns (based on code references and migrations):

- **id** (UUID, Primary Key)
- **client_id** (UUID, Foreign Key to clients table)
- **engagement_type** (TEXT) - Dropdown values: KOL, GTM, Advisory, Hybrid
- **scope** (TEXT) - Project description and regions
- **start_date** (DATE) - When engagement starts
- **milestones** (TEXT) - Expected milestones for the project
- **client_contacts** (TEXT) - Primary contacts on client side
- **holohive_contacts** (TEXT) - Primary contacts on Holo Hive side
- **telegram_url** (TEXT) - Telegram group link (recently added)

   166Ôćĺ  linked_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'opportunity', 'partner', 'affiliate'
   167Ôćĺ  opportunity_id UUID REFERENCES crm_opportunities(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   168Ôćĺ  partner_id UUID REFERENCES crm_partners(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   169Ôćĺ  affiliate_id UUID REFERENCES crm_affiliates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   170Ôćĺ
   171Ôćĺ  -- Role in this relationship
   172Ôćĺ  role TEXT,
   173Ôćĺ  is_primary BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   174Ôćĺ
   175Ôćĺ  created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   176Ôćĺ
   177Ôćĺ  -- Ensure only one link type is set
   178Ôćĺ  CONSTRAINT check_single_link CHECK (
   179Ôćĺ    (CASE WHEN opportunity_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
   180Ôćĺ     CASE WHEN partner_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END +
   181Ôćĺ     CASE WHEN affiliate_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 1
   182Ôćĺ  )
   183Ôćĺ);
   184Ôćĺ
   185Ôćĺ-- ============================================
   186Ôćĺ-- 6. STAGE HISTORY (For Analytics)
   187Ôćĺ-- Tracks all stage changes for conversion/velocity analysis
   188Ôćĺ-- ============================================
   189ÔćĺCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS crm_stage_history (
   190Ôćĺ  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   191Ôćĺ
   192Ôćĺ  -- Object reference
   193Ôćĺ  object_type TEXT NOT NULL, -- 'opportunity', 'partner', 'affiliate'
   194Ôćĺ  object_id UUID NOT NULL,
   195Ôćĺ
   196Ôćĺ  -- Stage change
   197Ôćĺ  from_stage TEXT,
   198Ôćĺ  to_stage TEXT NOT NULL,
   199Ôćĺ
   200Ôćĺ  -- Who and when
   201Ôćĺ  changed_by UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id),
   202Ôćĺ  changed_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE DEFAULT NOW(),
   203Ôćĺ
   204Ôćĺ  notes TEXT
   205Ôćĺ);
   206Ôćĺ
   207Ôćĺ-- ============================================
   208Ôćĺ-- INDEXES
   209Ôćĺ-- ============================================
   210Ôćĺ
   211Ôćĺ-- Opportunities
   212ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_stage ON crm_opportunities(stage);
   213ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_owner ON crm_opportunities(owner_id);
   214ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_source ON crm_opportunities(source);
   215ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_affiliate ON crm_opportunities(affiliate_id);
   216ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_opportunities_created ON crm_opportunities(created_at);
   217Ôćĺ
   218Ôćĺ-- Partners
   219ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_status ON crm_partners(status);
   220ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_category ON crm_partners(category);
   221ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_partners_owner ON crm_partners(owner_id);
   222Ôćĺ
   223Ôćĺ-- Affiliates
   224ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_status ON crm_affiliates(status);
   225ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_category ON crm_affiliates(category);
   226ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_affiliates_owner ON crm_affiliates(owner_id);
   227Ôćĺ
   228Ôćĺ-- Contacts
   229ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_contacts_owner ON crm_contacts(owner_id);
   230ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_contacts_email ON crm_contacts(email);
   231Ôćĺ
   232Ôćĺ-- Contact links
   233ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_contact ON crm_contact_links(contact_id);
   234ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_opportunity ON crm_contact_links(opportunity_id);
   235ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_partner ON crm_contact_links(partner_id);
   236ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_contact_links_affiliate ON crm_contact_links(affiliate_id);
   237Ôćĺ
   238Ôćĺ-- Stage history
   239ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_stage_history_object ON crm_stage_history(object_type, object_id);
   240ÔćĺCREATE INDEX idx_crm_stage_history_changed ON crm_stage_history(changed_at);
   241Ôćĺ
   242Ôćĺ-- ============================================
   243Ôćĺ-- COMMENTS
   244Ôćĺ-- ============================================
   245Ôćĺ
   246ÔćĺCOMMENT ON TABLE crm_opportunities IS 'Unified sales pipeline: Leads (new/contacted/qualified) Ôćĺ Deals (proposal/contract/closed). Single entity journey.';
   247ÔćĺCOMMENT ON TABLE crm_partners IS 'BD relationships. Categories: service_provider, investor_vc, project, individual. Can also be affiliates.';
   248ÔćĺCOMMENT ON TABLE crm_affiliates IS 'KOLs and referrers with commission/rev-share. Lifecycle: new Ôćĺ active Ôćĺ inactive.';
   249ÔćĺCOMMENT ON TABLE crm_contacts IS 'Individual people who can be linked to multiple CRM objects.';
   250ÔćĺCOMMENT ON TABLE crm_contact_links IS 'Links contacts to opportunities, partners, or affiliates with role info.';
   251ÔćĺCOMMENT ON TABLE crm_stage_history IS 'Audit trail of all stage changes for analytics (conversion rates, velocity).';
   252Ôćĺ
```

commit 816758ca79d5673742c90fa9c356b31c35bbdd12
Author: Andy Lee <andy@holohive.io>
Date:   Sat Jan 31 15:02:51 2026 +0900

    Add Client Tracker (Context, Decisions, Weekly Updates), Delivery Log, and KOL Pricing column
    
    ## Client Tracker ÔÇö 3 Sections (Admin + Portal)
    
    ### Context (Engagement Overview)
    - New `client_context` table (engagement type, scope, start date, milestones, contacts)
    - Admin: CRUD modal on client cards with Briefcase icon button
    - Portal: Styled card with gradient header, timeline milestones, side-by-side contacts
    
    ### Decisions & History (Enhanced Meeting Notes)
    - New `client_decision_log` table; added attendees & action_items to meeting notes
    - Admin: Meeting notes modal with Tabs (Notes / Decision Log), rich text action items
    - Portal: Meeting notes show attendees/action items; Decision Log section with purple accents
    
    ### Weekly Status (What's Active Now)
    - New `client_weekly_updates` table (focus, initiatives, next check-in, open questions)
    - Admin: CRUD modal on client cards with Activity icon button
    - Portal: Latest update card with expandable history
    
    ### Client Card Optimization
    - Collapsed Context, Updates, Notes, Delivery into compact 4-button icon grid row
    
    ## Client Delivery Log
    - New `client_delivery_log` table (authenticated-only, not visible on portal)
    - Dedicated page at /clients/[id]/delivery-log styled like campaign detail pages
    - Day number column (Day 0 = earliest entry), sortable asc/desc toggle
```

## Client Context Table - Current State Analysis

Based on my thorough investigation of the codebase, here's the complete picture:

### 1. Full Column List for client_context Table

The `client_context` table currently has the following columns (based on code references and migrations):

- **id** (UUID, Primary Key)
- **client_id** (UUID, Foreign Key to clients table)
- **engagement_type** (TEXT) - Dropdown values: KOL, GTM, Advisory, Hybrid
- **scope** (TEXT) - Project description and regions
- **start_date** (DATE) - When engagement starts
- **milestones** (TEXT) - Expected milestones for the project
- **client_contacts** (TEXT) - Primary contacts on client side
- **holohive_contacts** (TEXT) - Primary contacts on Holo Hive side
- **telegram_url** (TEXT) - Telegram group link (recently added)
- **shared_drive_url** (TEXT) - Google Drive/shared storage link (recently added)
- **gtm_sync_url** (TEXT) - GTM tracker/sync URL (recently added)
- **created_at** (TIMESTAMP)
- **updated_at** (TIMESTAMP)

**Note:** There is NO `onboarding_phase` column currently in the table. The user mentioned it was "just added via migration," but I cannot find that migration or any references to it in the codebase.

### 2. Recent Additions

The three URL fields (`telegram_url`, `shared_drive_url`, `gtm_sync_url`) were recently added in the current uncommitted changes (git diff HEAD shows these additions). These are displayed in the admin interface under a "Resource Links" section.

  .from('client_context')

  .eq('client_id', clientId)
  .single();
```

**Fetch Method:** Uses public Supabase client to fetch a single client_context record by client_id.

### 4. ClientContext Type Definition - app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx

```typescript

## 2. **`handleContextSubmit` Function (Lines 668-695)**

```typescript
const handleContextSubmit = async () => {
  if (!contextModalClient) return;
  const existing = clientContexts[contextModalClient.id];
  const payload = {
    client_id: contextModalClient.id,
    engagement_type: contextForm.engagement_type || null,
    scope: contextForm.scope || null,
    start_date: contextForm.start_date ? contextForm.start_date.toISOString().split('T')[0] : null,
    milestones: contextForm.milestones || null,
    client_contacts: contextForm.client_contacts || null,
    holohive_contacts: contextForm.holohive_contacts || null,
    telegram_url: contextForm.telegram_url || null,
    shared_drive_url: contextForm.shared_drive_url || null,
    gtm_sync_url: contextForm.gtm_sync_url || null,
    updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
  };
  try {
    if (existing) {
      await supabase.from('client_context').update(payload).eq('id', existing.id);
    } else {
      await supabase.from('client_context').insert(payload);
    }
    await refreshClientContexts();
    setContextModalClient(null);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error saving context:', err);
  }
};
```

---

- **Scope textarea** (lines 2673-2684): Conditional read-only or editable based on linked CRM account
- **Start Date calendar picker** (lines 2686-2710): Conditional read-only or date picker
- **Milestones textarea** (line 2712-2714)
- **Client Contacts input** (line 2716-2718)
- **Holo Hive Contacts input** (line 2720-2722)

## 2. **`handleContextSubmit` Function (Lines 668-695)**

```typescript
const handleContextSubmit = async () => {
  if (!contextModalClient) return;
  const existing = clientContexts[contextModalClient.id];
  const payload = {
    client_id: contextModalClient.id,
    engagement_type: contextForm.engagement_type || null,
    scope: contextForm.scope || null,
    start_date: contextForm.start_date ? contextForm.start_date.toISOString().split('T')[0] : null,
    milestones: contextForm.milestones || null,
    client_contacts: contextForm.client_contacts || null,
    holohive_contacts: contextForm.holohive_contacts || null,
    telegram_url: contextForm.telegram_url || null,
    shared_drive_url: contextForm.shared_drive_url || null,
    gtm_sync_url: contextForm.gtm_sync_url || null,
    updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
  };
  try {
    if (existing) {
      await supabase.from('client_context').update(payload).eq('id', existing.id);
    } else {
      await supabase.from('client_context').insert(payload);
    }
    await refreshClientContexts();
    setContextModalClient(null);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error saving context:', err);
  }
};
```

---

# Plan: Progressive Disclosure Portal ÔÇö Timeline, Action Board, Campaign DNA, Live Status

## Context
The client portal currently shows the same layout regardless of onboarding stage. The spec calls for a "progressive disclosure" experience that evolves from a getting-started view (State 1) through discovery (State 2) to a full campaign tracker (State 3). This involves adding a 7-day timeline, an Action Board, bringing back Campaign DNA, adding live KOL status metrics, and gating visibility by phase.

## Files to Modify
1. `app/public/portal/[id]/page.tsx` ÔÇö all portal UI changes + state logic + new fetch for `onboarding_phase`
2. `app/clients/page.tsx` ÔÇö add `onboarding_phase` dropdown to admin context form

## DB Change (already done)

- Values: `NULL` (auto/time-based), `'kickoff'`, `'discovery'`, `'tracker'`

Update `fetchClientContext` select query to include `onboarding_phase`.

Add computed phase logic (after all data is loaded):
\```
computedPhase:

    daysSinceSubmission = days since earliest form_responses.submitted_at for this client

## Step 3: 7-Day Timeline Component

**Position:** After Welcome Section, before Onboarding Banner

A horizontal tracker with 4 milestone nodes connected by lines (like a delivery tracker):

Visual states per node:

- **Active (current):** Pulsing teal ring, label bold
- **Locked:** Gray circle, dashed gray line, muted text

Show always (all phases). In `kickoff` phase, only node 1 is active, rest locked. After form submission, node 1 completes and node 2 activates. In `tracker`, all complete.
